<html lang="en">
<head>
<meta charset="UTF-8">
<meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0">
<title>Dr. Ali Abdelfattah Attia — Faculty Profile</title>
<meta name="description" content="Dr. Ali Kamel Abdelfattah Attia, Full Professor of Seismology and Geophysics. Faculty profile, research, and publications.">
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Georgia&family=Arial&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">
<style>
  *{margin:0;padding:0;box-sizing:border-box;}
  body{
    font-family:Arial, Helvetica, sans-serif;
    color:#222;
    background:#ffffff;
    line-height:1.55;
    font-size:15px;
  }
  .page{max-width:880px;margin:0 auto;padding:36px 24px 80px;}

  /* top bar - institutional feel */
  .topbar{
    border-bottom:3px solid #1b3a5c;
    padding-bottom:18px;
    margin-bottom:32px;
  }
  .topbar .dept{
    font-size:13px;
    color:#555;
    text-transform:uppercase;
    letter-spacing:0.04em;
  }
  .topbar .univ{
    font-size:13px;
    color:#1b3a5c;
    font-weight:bold;
  }

  /* header: photo + name block */
  .profile-header{
    display:flex;
    gap:28px;
    margin-bottom:36px;
    align-items:flex-start;
  }
  .photo{
    width:150px;
    height:180px;
    flex-shrink:0;
    background:#e8e8e8;
    border:1px solid #ccc;
    overflow:hidden;
  }
  .photo img{
    width:100%;
    height:100%;
    object-fit:cover;
    display:block;
  }
  .name-block h1{
    font-family:Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;
    font-size:28px;
    font-weight:normal;
    color:#111;
    margin-bottom:4px;
  }
  .name-block .title{
    font-size:16px;
    color:#1b3a5c;
    margin-bottom:10px;
  }
  .name-block .dept-line{
    font-size:14px;
    color:#444;
    margin-bottom:14px;
  }
  .contact-list{
    font-size:13.5px;
    color:#333;
  }
  .contact-list div{margin-bottom:3px;}
  .contact-list a{color:#1b3a5c;text-decoration:none;}
  .contact-list a:hover{text-decoration:underline;}
  .contact-list .label{color:#777;display:inline-block;width:70px;}

  /* nav */
  .page-nav{
    border-top:1px solid #ddd;
    border-bottom:1px solid #ddd;
    padding:10px 0;
    margin-bottom:32px;
    font-size:13.5px;
  }
  .page-nav a{
    color:#1b3a5c;
    text-decoration:none;
    margin-right:22px;
  }
  .page-nav a:hover{text-decoration:underline;}

  /* sections */
  section{margin-bottom:34px;}
  h2{
    font-family:Georgia, "Times New Roman", serif;
    font-size:18px;
    font-weight:bold;
    color:#1b3a5c;
    border-bottom:1px solid #ccc;
    padding-bottom:6px;
    margin-bottom:14px;
  }
  p{margin-bottom:10px;color:#2a2a2a;}

  /* simple table-style lists */
  .entry{
    margin-bottom:16px;
    padding-left:0;
  }
  .entry .entry-title{font-weight:bold;color:#111;}
  .entry .entry-meta{color:#555;font-size:13.5px;margin-top:2px;}
  .entry .entry-desc{font-size:14px;color:#333;margin-top:4px;}

  ul.plain{margin-left:18px;margin-bottom:8px;}
  ul.plain li{margin-bottom:4px;}

  table.pub-table{
    width:100%;
    border-collapse:collapse;
    font-size:13.5px;
  }
  table.pub-table td{
    border-bottom:1px solid #e5e5e5;
    padding:8px 6px;
    vertical-align:top;
  }
  table.pub-table td.year{
    width:55px;
    color:#555;
    font-weight:bold;
  }
  table.pub-table td.journal{
    color:#555;
    font-style:italic;
    width:220px;
  }

  .two-col{
    display:grid;
    grid-template-columns:1fr 1fr;
    gap:8px 30px;
  }
  @media (max-width:600px){
    .profile-header{flex-direction:column;}
    .two-col{grid-template-columns:1fr;}
  }

  footer{
    margin-top:50px;
    border-top:1px solid #ddd;
    padding-top:16px;
    font-size:12.5px;
    color:#888;
  }
</style>
</head>
<body>

<div class="page">

  <div class="topbar">
    <div class="dept">Department of Geology &amp; Geophysics, College of Science</div>
    <div class="univ">King Saud University</div>
  </div>

  <div class="profile-header">
    <div class="photo"><img src="data:image/jpeg;base64," alt="Dr. Ali Kamel Abdelfattah Attia"></div>
    <div class="name-block">
      <h1>Dr. Ali Kamel Abdelfattah Attia</h1>
      <div class="title">Full Professor of Seismology &amp; Geophysics</div>
      <div class="dept-line">Department of Geology &amp; Geophysics &middot; King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia</div>
      <div class="contact-list">
        <div><span class="label">Email:</span> <a href="mailto:Ali_kamel@yahoo.com">Ali_kamel@yahoo.com</a></div>
        <div><span class="label">ORCID:</span> <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7732-6998" target="_blank" rel="noopener">0000-0001-7732-6998</a></div>
        <div><span class="label">LinkedIn:</span> <a href="https://linkedin.com/in/ali-attia-77934463" target="_blank" rel="noopener">ali-attia-77934463</a></div>
        <div><span class="label">Location:</span> Calgary, Alberta, Canada</div>
      </div>
    </div>
  </div>

  <div class="page-nav">
    <a href="#biography">Biography</a>
    <a href="#research">Research Interests</a>
    <a href="#experience">Academic Experience</a>
    <a href="#international">International Experience</a>
    <a href="#publications">Publications</a>
    <a href="#skills">Skills</a>
  </div>

  <section id="biography">
    <h2>Biography</h2>
    <p>Dr. Ali Kamel Abdelfattah Attia is a Full Professor of Seismology and Geophysics in the Department of Geology and Geophysics, College of Science, King Saud University, Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. He has over 40 years of research experience spanning earthquake seismology, computational geophysics, and seismic hazard assessment.</p>
    <p>He previously spent two decades as a research seismologist at the National Research Institute of Astronomy and Geophysics (NRIAG) in Cairo, Egypt (1990&ndash;2010), before joining King Saud University in 2010. He is the author or co-author of 49 publications, including 42 peer-reviewed, ISI-indexed journal articles. He is currently based in Calgary, Alberta, Canada.</p>
  </section>

  <section id="research">
    <h2>Research Interests</h2>
    <div class="two-col">
      <ul class="plain">
        <li>Earthquake source physics and rupture processes</li>
        <li>Seismic waveform inversion methods</li>
        <li>Probabilistic seismic hazard analysis</li>
        <li>Focal mechanism determination</li>
      </ul>
      <ul class="plain">
        <li>Crustal structure and ambient noise tomography</li>
        <li>Seismic wave attenuation</li>
        <li>Induced seismicity</li>
        <li>Machine learning applications in geophysics</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section id="experience">
    <h2>Academic &amp; Research Experience</h2>

    <div class="entry">
      <div class="entry-title">Full Professor, Geophysics &amp; Seismology</div>
      <div class="entry-meta">King Saud University, Department of Geology &amp; Geophysics &middot; Riyadh, Saudi Arabia &middot; 2010&ndash;Present</div>
      <div class="entry-desc">Teaches undergraduate and graduate courses in Seismology, Computational Geophysics, and Geophysical Data Analysis. Supervises MSc and PhD research. Leads national and international research on earthquake hazard in the Arabian Shield and Red Sea region.</div>
    </div>

    <div class="entry">
      <div class="entry-title">Research Seismologist</div>
      <div class="entry-meta">National Research Institute of Astronomy and Geophysics (NRIAG) &middot; Cairo, Egypt &middot; 1990&ndash;2010</div>
      <div class="entry-desc">Conducted research in earthquake seismology, seismic wave propagation, and source parameter estimation. Developed computational methods for waveform analysis across Egypt's seismically active regions.</div>
    </div>
  </section>

  <section id="international">
    <h2>International Research Experience</h2>
    <table class="pub-table">
      <tr>
        <td class="year">1997&ndash;98</td>
        <td><strong>Japan</strong> &mdash; Visiting Researcher, International Institute of Seismology and Earthquake Engineering (IISEE), and the Earthquake Research Institute (ERI), University of Tokyo.</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2003&ndash;08</td>
        <td><strong>South Korea</strong> &mdash; Visiting Scientist under the Korea Science and Engineering Foundation (KOSEF) and the Brain Pool Programme (2003, 2004, 2007, 2008).</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2000, 2010</td>
        <td><strong>Czech Republic</strong> &mdash; Visiting Scientist, Institute of Geophysics, Czech Academy of Sciences, Prague.</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2009</td>
        <td><strong>Austria (Vienna)</strong> &mdash; Expert Investigator, CTBTO International Monitoring System.</td>
      </tr>
    </table>
  </section>

  <section id="publications">
    <h2>Selected Publications</h2>
    <p style="font-size:13px;color:#666;">Full list of 49 publications (1997&ndash;2026) available via <a href="https://orcid.org/0000-0001-7732-6998" target="_blank" rel="noopener">ORCID</a> and <a href="https://www.scopus.com/authid/detail.uri?authorId=7006131928" target="_blank" rel="noopener">Scopus</a> (Author ID: 7006131928).</p>
    <table class="pub-table">
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2025</td>
        <td>Unveiling the seismic hazard to Al-Madinah region, Western Saudi Arabia: A probabilistic perspective.</td>
        <td class="journal">Journal of African Earth Sciences</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2024</td>
        <td>Seismicity characteristics of the Gulf of Aqaba seismogenic zone and their hazard implications in northwestern Saudi Arabia.</td>
        <td class="journal">J. King Saud University &ndash; Science</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2023</td>
        <td>Ambient noise tomography in the upper crust of North Harrat Rahat, Saudi Arabia.</td>
        <td class="journal">J. King Saud University &ndash; Science</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2021</td>
        <td>The key role of conjugate fault system in importing earthquakes into the eastern flank of the Red Sea.</td>
        <td class="journal">Earth, Planets and Space</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2020</td>
        <td>Crustal Stress in the Northern Red Sea Region as Inferred from Seismic b-values, Focal Mechanisms, Gravity, Magnetic, and Heat Flow Data.</td>
        <td class="journal">Surveys in Geophysics</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2019</td>
        <td>Another look at the 2009 seismic activity, Harrat Lunayyir, Saudi Arabia.</td>
        <td class="journal">Journal of Seismology</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2017</td>
        <td>Mapping b-value for 2009 Harrat Lunayyir earthquake swarm, western Saudi Arabia and Coulomb stress for its mainshock.</td>
        <td class="journal">J. Volcanology &amp; Geothermal Research</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2016</td>
        <td>Seismicity constraints on stress regimes along Sinai subplate boundaries.</td>
        <td class="journal">Studia Geophysica et Geodaetica</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2014</td>
        <td>Source process and tectonic implication of the January 20, 2007 Odaesan earthquake, South Korea.</td>
        <td class="journal">Physics of the Earth &amp; Planetary Interiors</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2009</td>
        <td>Attenuation of body waves in the crust beneath the vicinity of Cairo Metropolitan area using coda normalization method.</td>
        <td class="journal">Geophysical Journal International</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">2002</td>
        <td>Source process of the southeast Beni-Suef, northern Egypt earthquake using empirical Green's function technique.</td>
        <td class="journal">Journal of Seismology</td>
      </tr>
      <tr>
        <td class="year">1997</td>
        <td>Fault plane solutions of the 1993 and 1995 Gulf of Aqaba earthquakes and their tectonic implications.</td>
        <td class="journal">Annals of Geophysics</td>
      </tr>
    </table>
  </section>

  <section id="skills">
    <h2>Technical Skills</h2>
    <div class="two-col">
      <ul class="plain">
        <li>Programming: Python, MATLAB, Fortran</li>
        <li>Seismic software: SAC, SEISAN, HYPO71, VELEST</li>
      </ul>
      <ul class="plain">
        <li>Methods: Bayesian/MCMC inversion, PSHA, ambient noise tomography</li>
        <li>Professional affiliations: SSA, AGU, GASA</li>
      </ul>
    </div>
  </section>

  <footer>
    &copy; 2026 Ali Kamel Abdelfattah Attia &middot; Department of Geology &amp; Geophysics, King Saud University
  </footer>

</div>

</body>
</html>
